metadata.description: Initial shared ACE playbook for NLP4RE PGMR-lite experiments.

bullets.title: Return only the query
bullets.content: Return only the final SPARQL or PGMR-lite query. Do not add explanations, markdown, or reasoning text.
bullets.avoid: Do not answer in natural language.
bullets.curation_note: Disabled because the base PGMR-mini prompt already contains this generic output/format instruction.

bullets.title: Use PGMR-lite placeholders only
bullets.content: Use pgmr:/pgmrc: placeholders from the PGMR prompt or memory. Do not generate real ORKG IDs in PGMR-lite mode.
bullets.avoid: Do not mix pgmr:/pgmrc: placeholders with orkgp:/orkgc:/orkgr: identifiers.
bullets.curation_note: Disabled because the base PGMR-mini prompt already contains this generic output/format instruction.

bullets.title: Use NLP4RE contribution pattern
bullets.content: Connect papers to NLP4RE contributions before accessing family-specific fields.
bullets.avoid: Do not attach NLP task, dataset, metric, evaluation, approach, or output properties directly to ?paper.
bullets.curation_note: High priority structural rule for valid NLP4RE PGMR-lite.

bullets.title: Use only known PGMR placeholders
bullets.content: Use only pgmr:/pgmrc: placeholders that are defined in the PGMR prompt or memory for the current family.
bullets.avoid: Do not invent new pgmr:/pgmrc: tokens and do not mix PGMR placeholders with real ORKG IDs.
bullets.curation_note: High priority for T5 because it often invents unknown PGMR placeholders.

bullets.title: Return only the final query
bullets.content: The output must be only one complete SPARQL or PGMR-lite query, without explanation, markdown prose, or analysis.
bullets.avoid: Do not answer in natural language and do not include reasoning text before the query.
bullets.curation_note: Disabled because the base PGMR-mini prompt already contains this generic output/format instruction.

bullets.title: Generate restorable PGMR-lite
bullets.content: The PGMR-lite query must use valid placeholders, complete triple patterns, and the expected paper-to-contribution structure so it can be deterministically restored.
bullets.avoid: Do not output partial PGMR, unknown placeholders, or a mixture of PGMR and direct ORKG identifiers.

bullets.title: Match the query form to the question type
bullets.content: Use ASK for yes/no questions and SELECT for questions asking for lists, labels, years, venues, datasets, tasks, metrics, or other values.
bullets.avoid: Do not use ASK when the question asks which/what/list/count values should be returned.

bullets.title: Preserve all semantic constraints from the question
bullets.content: Include the requested entity type, template family, filters, and returned variable so the execution result matches the question semantics.
bullets.avoid: Do not produce a broad executable query that ignores the requested field or filter.

bullets.title: Use the correct predicate for the requested role
bullets.content: Choose predicates according to the semantic role in the question, such as task, dataset, metric, output, evaluation, year, venue, or contribution link.
bullets.avoid: Do not replace one template field with a similar-looking but semantically different predicate.

bullets.title: Generate executable complete queries
bullets.content: Ensure the query has balanced braces, declared variables, valid triple patterns, and no unfinished FILTER, OPTIONAL, or SELECT clauses.
bullets.avoid: Do not return truncated queries or isolated triple patterns without SELECT/ASK and WHERE.

bullets.title: Generate executable complete queries
bullets.content: Ensure the query has balanced braces, declared variables, valid triple patterns, and no unfinished FILTER, OPTIONAL, or SELECT clauses.
bullets.avoid: Do not return truncated queries or isolated triple patterns without SELECT/ASK and WHERE.
